message:
fix(q): validate quick-capture issues before persisting

`br q` (quick-capture) was building an `Issue` and handing it directly
to `storage.create_issue`, skipping the `IssueValidator` that all
other create paths run through. The DB column constraints catch the
worst cases (NOT NULL, unique IDs), but the application-level field
limits — e.g. the 500-character cap on `title` — were silently
violated, persisting an over-long title to disk and then failing only
when something downstream tried to render or sync it.

Fix: call `IssueValidator::validate(&issue)` between `compute_-
content_hash` and `storage.create_issue`. On failure the validator
errors are mapped to `BeadsError::from_validation_errors`, the CLI
reports them, and nothing reaches storage.

E2E coverage: `q_rejects_overlong_title_without_persisting` in
`tests/e2e_quick_capture.rs` shells out to `br q <501-char title>`,
asserts the command fails with an error mentioning the title and the
"exceeds 500" length cap, then runs `br list --json` and asserts the
issue list is empty. This pins both the user-facing error shape and
the no-persistence guarantee.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
